title: Shalam
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:Shalam-EGTW.jpg]] |name=Shalam |region=*[[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Shalam system/Legends|Shalam system]] *[[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]] |sector= |system=[[Shalam system/Legends|Shalam system]] |coordinates=M-7 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial{{StoryCite|story=The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}} |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain= |water= |interest= |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies= |socialgroup=[[Shalamite]] |language= |government=[[Monarchy/Legends|Monarchy]]{{CSWECite|I|323|Gareth, King}} |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation=[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Shalam system/Legends|Shalam system]] }} {{Quote|Treaty signings bore me, and besides that worthless son of a slorth Gareth tried to cheat me at Laro last time we were there.|Han Solo, about Shalam|}} '''Shalam''' was an [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was home to the [[Shalamite]] cultural group. It was subjected to a [[Bombardment of Shalam|bombardment]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emperor's Disciple]]'' and had become a part of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] by [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]]. [[19 ABY/Legends|Seven years]] later, the Shalamite had imposed a one-hundred-percent tariff on the import of the [[Jandarra]] [[Vegetable/Legends|vegetable]] from the planet [[Jubilar/Legends|Jubilar]]. The New Republic [[Politics/Legends|politician]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] made at least two [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] visits to Shalam, and in 19 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], [[Mayor/Legends|Mayor]] [[Incavi Larado|Incavi Baker]] of Jubilar's [[City/Legends|city]] [[Death (town)|Death]] made an offer of a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] run to Shalam to [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], Organa Solo's [[Marriage/Legends|husband]] who was posing as a visiting independent [[Merchant/Legends|trader]], as a way to lead him into an ambush by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. ==Description== Shalam was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]{{CSWECite|III|143|Shalam}} located in the [[Shalam system/Legends|Shalam system]], a part of the [[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]] portion of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]. ==History== {{Quote|Look, Chewie's gone home and won't be back for another few weeks, my wife and kids are off—''"
"''—the Shalamite trip. Right. Why didn't you go?|Han Solo and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|}} At some point between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} establishes the [[bombardment of Shalam]] took place before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emperor's Disciple]]'' of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Ishin-Il-Raz]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] carried out a [[bombardment of Shalam]]. By [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], Shalam fell within the borders of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The [[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' New Republic [[Politics/Legends|politician]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] and her [[Marriage/Legends|husband]], the Human New Republic [[General/Legends|general]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], visited Shalam at one point by [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the main events of "[[The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett]]," during which Shalam is mentioned, to [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]]. with Han playing the [[laro]] card game with the planet's [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gareth (king)|Gareth]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Organa Solo again traveled to Shalam on a two-[[Standard week/Legends|week]]-long treaty signing visit. She took her [[Solo family/Legends|children]] with her—reasoning it would be a useful experience for them—as well as her [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]].{{CSWECite|I|101-102|C-3PO (See-Threepio)}} Despite her attempts to persuade her [[Gender/Legends|husband]] to accompany them, he refused the offer. [[File:The_Last_One_Standing_by_Chris_Trevas.jpg|thumb|right|300px|A confrontation between Boba Fett (left) and Han Solo, into which the latter was lured with an offer of a supposed smuggling run to Shalam]] By that time, intending to capitalize on the value of the [[Foodstuff/Legends|delicacy]] [[Vegetable/Legends|vegetable]]{{CSWECite|II|137|jandarra}} [[Jandarra]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Jubilar system/Legends|Jubilar system]] planet [[Jubilar/Legends|Jubilar]], Shalam's [[Shalamite]] social group had imposed a one-hundred-percent tariff on its import. During Leia's visit to Shalam, Han—who was being nostalgic for his prior [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] career—left the New Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and traveled to Jubilar, which he had visited [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] prior and where he posed as "Luke Morgavi," an independent [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] looking for a smuggling job. [[Mayor/Legends|Mayor]] [[Incavi Larado|Incavi Baker]] of{{CSWECite|I|55|Baker, Mayor}} the local [[City/Legends|city]] [[Death (town)|Death]], who was also an informant for the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], then learned of "Morgavi's" arrival and, recognizing Solo, notified Fett about it. In order to help arrange for an ambush opportunity for the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]], Baker had an [[Aide/Legends|aide]] approach Solo and offer him a deal involving the smuggling of a cargo of Jandarra to Shalam. Due to the irony of his wife being on that planet on official business at the time, coupled with the fact Jandarra was among Leia's favorite treats, Solo nearly laughed out loud at the offer. He further imagined even Leia would be amused at him arriving at Shalam with a cargo of the vegetable, and the Shalamite would not dare to prosecute him over the infraction. During the subsequent meeting between Solo and Baker, the former realized it was a trap, and during the ensuing [[Lightfight|firefight]], Fett accidentally [[Death/Legends|fatally]] shot the mayor, with both Solo and the bounty hunter ultimately surviving the encounter. ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|Jandarra. ''[…]'' After almost two years of work we managed to cultivate them—''"
"''And the Shalamite slapped a 100% tariff on you.|Incavi Baker and Han Solo, posing as "Luke Morgavi"|}} Shalam was governed by a [[Monarchy/Legends|monarchy]] and was home to a social group known as the Shalamite, who maintained a [[Shalamite Navy|space navy]]. ==Behind the scenes== Shalam was first mentioned in "[[The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett]]," a short story by [[Daniel Keys Moran]] that was published in the [[1996]] anthology ''[[Tales of the Bounty Hunters]]''. [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' erroneously claimed that Han Solo and Gareth played a game of laro on Coruscant instead of on Shalam. The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Shalam [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square M-7, thereby also placing it relatively far from Jubilar, which overrode ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia''{{'s}} characterization of the worlds as being "nearby." ==Appearances== {{Indexpage}} *{{StoryCite|story=The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}} {{1stm}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Shalam}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Shalam }} [[Category:New Republic planets]] [[Category:Planets in the Inner Rim]] [[Category:Planets in the Northern Dependencies]] [[Category:Terrestrial planets]]
